methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $10.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Quantum Computing. Range: $2.0B - $11.0B.

private.name: Photonic Inc

data.narrative:
Quantinuum, a Series E quantum computing company, is valued at $10.0B post-money following $625M in total capital raised. The valuation analysis triangulates against a peer universe of 10 comparable companies across quantum computing, comprising 3 public firms (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 7 private players including Infleqtion and Q-CTRL.

Two valuation methods apply to this stage and sector. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to the $10.0B last post-money valuation with a 1.4x multiplier typical for Series E advancement, implies $14.0B enterprise value. Peer-median analysis across the three public quantum computing comparables yields a median valuation of $5.5B, with the peer range spanning $2.0B (D-Wave) to $11.0B (IonQ). The divergence between these approaches reflects heterogeneity in market positioning and technology maturity within the sector.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $4.7B (low) to $16.1B (high), with a median of $9.8B. The current $10.0B valuation sits near the triangulated median, suggesting reasonable calibration to peer multiples and stage dynamics. Key uncertainties include the timing and success of commercialization relative to competing quantum approaches, and whether the peer set adequately reflects differentiation in Quantinuum's technical architecture and partnership profile.
